hero.label: Made for Truewind
hero.headline: I want to build GTM motion for the accounting platform startups deserve.
hero.subheadline: Truewind is solving a real problem for founders—turning accounting from a monthly headache into an automated, error-free process. I've spent the last year learning how to acquire startup customers (at Copygen) and how they actually buy (at NextAfter). Now I want to help Alex and the team prove that AI-powered accounting is the category that changes how fast-growing companies scale.

whyCompany.title: Solving a founder pain point with AI that actually works
whyCompany.body: Truewind reduces accounting time by 65% through automated transaction classification and reconciliation. This isn't a nice-to-have for startups, it's operational breathing room. I've talked to enough founders (and been around them as a Division II athlete with startup employees) to know that finance automation is the unsexy category with massive demand.

whyCompany.title: 2026 is the year Truewind scales from 100 to 1000 customers
whyCompany.body: The company is already trusted by accounting firms like EisnerAmper and Frank Rimerman, raised $17M, and has 30 strong operators. This GTM Associate role exists because the founding team (two-time YC founders, engineers from Flexport and Pinterest) is ready to move fast. Being here now means building the playbook that works, not inheriting one.

whyCompany.title: Direct path from BDR cold outreach to startup GTM strategy
whyCompany.body: At Copygen, I ran cold outreach and LinkedIn sequences that helped acquire 15-20 paying clients. At NextAfter, I learned how B2B SaaS companies actually sell to other startups. GTM Associate at Truewind means applying both skills: finding early customers, understanding what they need, and shaping the messaging that makes them move.

quote: I've cold-called enough founders to know what keeps them up at night. I want to be the person who helps Truewind show them the solution.
